title: Origin of Baseball
content: Baseball developed in the United States during the 19th century from earlier bat-and-ball games. While the exact origins remain debated, Alexander Cartwright is widely credited with formalizing many of the game's modern rules in 1845, including the diamond-shaped field and foul lines. Baseball quickly became America's national pastime and later evolved into one of the world's most popular professional sports.

title: World Series
content: The World Series is the championship series of Major League Baseball (MLB), first played in 1903 between the champions of the American League and National League. The New York Yankees are the most successful franchise in World Series history with 27 championships. Winning the World Series is considered the highest achievement in professional baseball.

title: Babe Ruth
content: George Herman 'Babe' Ruth is one of the greatest players in baseball history. Nicknamed the 'Sultan of Swat,' he hit 714 career home runs, a record that stood for nearly four decades. Ruth played most of his career with the New York Yankees and helped transform baseball into one of America's most popular spectator sports during the early 20th century.

title: Jackie Robinson
content: Jackie Robinson made history on April 15, 1947, when he became the first African American player in the modern era of Major League Baseball, debuting for the Brooklyn Dodgers. His courage and outstanding performances helped break baseball's color barrier and inspired greater racial integration across professional sports. Robinson's jersey number 42 has been retired across all MLB teams.

title: Shohei Ohtani
content: Shohei Ohtani of Japan is widely regarded as one of the most unique players in baseball history because of his exceptional ability to excel as both a pitcher and a designated hitter. His two-way performances have drawn comparisons to the early career of Babe Ruth. In 2023, Ohtani signed a record-breaking 10-year contract with the Los Angeles Dodgers, reflecting his extraordinary value to the sport.

title: Major League Baseball
content: Major League Baseball (MLB) is the highest level of professional baseball in the United States and Canada. Founded in 1903 through the cooperation of the American League and National League, MLB currently consists of 30 teams. The league oversees the regular season, playoffs, and the World Series while promoting baseball globally through international events and player development initiatives.

title: National Baseball Hall of Fame
content: The National Baseball Hall of Fame and Museum, located in Cooperstown, New York, honors players, managers, umpires, and executives who have made significant contributions to baseball. Established in 1936, it preserves the history of the sport through exhibits, historical artifacts, and the induction of baseball's greatest figures. Election to the Hall of Fame is considered one of baseball's highest honors.

title: Basic Rules of Baseball
content: A baseball game is played between two teams of nine players over nine innings. Teams alternate between batting and fielding, with the objective of scoring runs by advancing around four bases and returning to home plate. A team records an out by striking out a batter, catching a batted ball before it touches the ground, or forcing a runner out at a base. The team with the most runs at the end of the game wins.
